title: 有了 agent 这种范式之后，语言大模型就不会算错基本的计算题了
content: With the agent paradigm, large language models no longer miscalculate basic arithmetic. Recognizing a problem as a calculation, the LLM writes a script on the spot to compute the answer instead of relying on character prediction, eliminating earlier errors. Consequently, many issues that were previously problematic for character‑prediction approaches are now resolved.

title: 这个时代的初级工程师如何学习?
content: Summarize learning steps for junior engineers: use AI to quickly grasp a system’s architecture and functionality, then test yourself with AI-generated questions to confirm understanding. Study the system’s business purpose, vision, and product roadmap, discussing with AI for critique and with teammates for broader context. Finally, propose improvements based on technical and product insights, validate ideas with AI before presenting to colleagues. This approach ensures both technical ownership and business alignment.

title: 突发：OpenAI大规模重组，总裁Brockman夺权挂帅
content: OpenAI announced a sudden, large‑scale reorganization before its IPO: the ChatGPT, Codex, and API teams were merged into one product org, and Greg Brockman, the co‑founder and former president, will now lead product strategy. Nick Turley, the former “father” of ChatGPT, was moved away from consumer products, and Ashley Alexander took his place. The new structure aims to build a “Super App” combining chat, coding, and browsing tools, while the company faces steep competition from Anthropic and Google and a rapid loss of senior staff.

title: 这届学生不行啊！普林斯顿被迫放弃坚守133年的传统，重新开始监考 | 原文
content: Princeton University will end its 133‑year “honor system” exam policy on July 1 2026. From that date, all in‑person tests must be supervised by faculty. The change follows a historical tradition that began in 1893 when students petitioned to remove instructor oversight, trusting each other to uphold academic integrity. The new policy marks the first time Princeton will require proctors for face‑to‑face examinations.

title: AI打工人的“第一笔工资”：16.88美元
content: Codex, an AI coding tool, earned $16.88 for a user by locating an open‑source security‑audit bounty, submitting a pull request, and navigating GitHub’s verification process—a task that took about 22 hours. The user paid a $20/​month subscription and used 22 million tokens. While the earnings suggest a potential monthly income of roughly $506 ($3,441 CNY), costs, token usage, and the irregular supply of suitable tasks dampen realistic profit expectations. The case shows AI can close a task‑to‑payment loop, but broader adoption faces logistical, ethical, and security hurdles.

title: SpaceXAI 合并后流失逾 50 名核心员工，预训练团队仅剩数人
content: SpaceXAI, formerly xAI, has lost more than 50 key researchers and engineers since its merger and rebranding, leaving only a handful in the pre‑training team. Departures include leaders in critical tech areas, many moving to Meta and Thinking Machine Labs. The exodus is blamed on organizational upheaval, Musk’s “extreme” work culture, unrealistic deadlines, and attractive equity liquidity. The talent drain undermines confidence in SpaceXAI’s R&D, threatening progress on its main model, Grok, and illustrating challenges of Musk‑style management in AI talent retention.

The foundation confirmed the funds were used for its activities. Alipay noted the account may have been shared with others, raised the possibility of illegal activity, and is working with police.

title: DeepSeek预测：未来三年，普通家庭升值最快的5项资产
content: This article argues that the fastest‑growing household assets in the next three years are non‑monetary: health, knowledge, emotional bonds, habits, and family values. Physical well‑being provides the most resilient foundation, while a family library and regular reading build the “cognitive asset” needed to outpace AI‑driven competition. Strong emotional capital protects against shocks, and well‑chosen daily habits generate long‑term benefits. Finally, enduring family traditions serve as an intangible, multigenerational asset that safeguards wealth beyond material gains.

title: 马尔代夫发生史上最严重单次潜水事故：5名游客身亡，包括海洋生物学家、前潜水教练等；遗体在约50米深水下洞穴被发现，疑因“氧气中毒” | 原文
content: Five Italian tourists died during a dive in the Maldives on 14 April, the worst single‑incident diving disaster in the country. The victims included an ocean‑biology professor and her daughter, an island‑monitoring specialist, and a former captain, all with significant marine experience. They were exploring a 50‑meter‑deep underwater cave on the Vavaʻu atoll when they failed to surface. Preliminary reports suggest the cause was oxygen toxicity.

content: The new models have upgraded tokenizers and higher compute costs, raising per‑task quota usage. Users notice slower performance in standard mode, prompting the release of a “fast” mode that doubles quota consumption. To mask the increase, the provider temporarily doubles quotas, making users adapt to higher usage before the price effectively rises. The strategy appears designed to avoid immediate backlash while shifting costs upward.

title: 特朗普和习近平究竟取得了什么成果？
content: Trump and Xi’s meetings produced several high‑level agreements, including cooperation on climate policy, a U.S. military–China defense dialogue framework, and a framework for trade dispute resolution. They also discussed joint investment initiatives in sustainable infrastructure and science, though many domestic concerns—such as China’s intellectual‑property conduct and Taiwan—remained unresolved. Overall, the talks yielded symbolic commitments rather than substantive policy breakthroughs.

title: Building a general-purpose accessibility agent—and what we learned in the process
content: GitHub is testing a general‑purpose accessibility agent to answer questions in Copilot and automatically fix simple accessibility issues in front‑end code. It has reviewed 3,535 PRs with a 68% resolution rate, targeting common problems like clear structure, proper naming, announcement awareness, text alternatives, and logical focus order. The agent uses a two‑sub‑agent design (passive reviewer and active implementer) with template schemas and linear steps to reduce token use and ensure accuracy. It escalates complex or high‑risk patterns to human teams and relies on manually logged accessibility issues for training.
